tests.description: Clicking the Import/Export sidebar link writes uxSettings.page through Chrome.set.
tests.key: App › changePage writes uxSettings.page when a sidebar link is clicked

tests.description: An external uxSettings.page change in storage navigates the rendered page via the onChanged listener.
tests.key: App › navigates when uxSettings changes in storage

tests.description: uxSettings.page selects which page renders; a non-HOME page shows its placeholder and hides the Home tab list.
tests.key: App › renders the page named by uxSettings.page

tests.description: renders the empty shell with the TabCommand logo and the placeholder content
tests.key: App › renders the sidebar and the Home page by default

tests.description: array-typed keys hydrate to [] when storage has no value
tests.key: Chrome › hydrates activeTabs/allUrls/previousLabels to [] when absent

tests.description: hash-typed keys hydrate to {} when storage has no value
tests.key: Chrome › hydrates labels/uxSettings/autoClosed to {} when absent

tests.description: present values are passed through untouched (no default applied)
tests.key: Chrome › passes through requested keys that are present

tests.description: remove delegates straight to chrome.storage.local.remove
tests.key: Chrome › remove delegates to chrome.storage.local.remove

tests.description: set delegates straight to chrome.storage.local.set
tests.key: Chrome › set delegates to chrome.storage.local.set

tests.description: previousLabels drops falsy entries and strips each entry's timestamp
tests.key: Chrome › strips timestamp and drops falsy previousLabels entries

tests.description: the single-string key form is supported and still hydrates its default
tests.key: Chrome › supports a single string key

tests.description: add wires document.onkeydown so a real keydown dispatches through trigger
tests.key: KeyDown › dispatches a real document keydown to subscribers

tests.description: the Cmd+F focus path: the metaKey + key event is forwarded intact
tests.key: KeyDown › forwards a Cmd+F event to subscribers

tests.description: every subscriber fires on a single trigger
tests.key: KeyDown › invokes all subscribers

tests.description: trigger fans the event out to a subscriber added via add
tests.key: KeyDown › invokes an added subscriber with the event

tests.description: remove unsubscribes a handler so later triggers skip it
tests.key: KeyDown › stops invoking a removed subscriber

tests.description: removing a function that was never added is a harmless no-op
tests.key: KeyDown › tolerates removing an unknown function

tests.description: removing a url drops it from the group's urlKeys in storage
tests.key: LabelCollection › removeUrl removes the url from the group urlKeys

tests.description: the group renders its title bar and a row for each member url
tests.key: LabelCollection › renders the group title and its member urls

tests.description: members split into active (have an open tab) above inactive (saved only)
tests.key: LabelCollection › splits members into active and inactive sections

tests.description: editing an existing label renames it, deleting the old map key and keeping urlKeys
tests.key: LabelForm › renames an existing label, deleting the old key

tests.description: submitting a new title writes a label with a derived color and position
tests.key: LabelForm › writes a new label with derived backgroundColor and position

tests.description: clicking the affordance expands the wrapper and reveals the LabelForm
tests.key: LabelFormContainer › expands to reveal the LabelForm when clicked

tests.description: the expand prop renders the form open on mount
tests.key: LabelFormContainer › renders expanded when the expand prop is set

tests.description: collapsed by default: the Add Group affordance shows but the form does not
tests.key: LabelFormContainer › shows the Add Group affordance and hides the form when collapsed

tests.description: the uxSettings-selected label is pinned in the selected section, not the grid
tests.key: Labels › pins the selected label above the grid

tests.description: every label in the map renders as a group card
tests.key: Labels › renders a group for each label

tests.description: with no labels the empty-state guidance and the Add Group CTA are shown
tests.key: Labels › shows the empty-state CTA when there are no labels

tests.description: the Home link writes uxSettings.page back to Home through Chrome.set
tests.key: Load › navigates home when the Home link is clicked

tests.description: the URL list renders one card per active tab, titled from the seeded url object
tests.key: Load › renders the active-tab URL list from storage

tests.description: an empty activeTabs list renders the page heading with no URL cards (no throw)
tests.key: Load › renders with no URL cards when there are no active tabs

tests.description: a processTotals storage change is handled without throwing and keeps rendering
tests.key: LoadMeter › handles a processTotals storage change

tests.description: mounting with seeded processTotals reads the value without throwing under jsdom (this exercises the jsdom guard around GradientPath — remove it and this fails)
tests.key: LoadMeter › reads seeded processTotals on mount without throwing

tests.description: the gauge renders its CPU and Memory labels
tests.key: LoadMeter › renders the gauge labels

tests.description: memory is shown in human-readable megabytes
tests.key: LoadProcesses › formats private memory in megabytes

tests.description: the listener detaches cleanly on unmount
tests.key: LoadProcesses › removes its listener on unmount without throwing

tests.description: a reported process renders its task title and CPU readout
tests.key: LoadProcesses › renders a row per process when the API emits

tests.description: empty by design until the chrome.processes API reports data (preview state)
tests.key: LoadProcesses › renders no process rows until the API emits

tests.description: a missing favicon falls back to the bundled default image
tests.key: LoadUrl › falls back to the default favicon when none is provided

tests.description: a tab with no sampled data shows only the title (no load readout)
tests.key: LoadUrl › omits the load readout when there is no sampled process data

tests.description: the card shows the tab title linked out to the derived url in a new tab
tests.key: LoadUrl › renders the title linked to the url

tests.description: a tab with sampled process data shows its CPU/memory load readout + bar
tests.key: LoadUrl › shows the per-tab load readout when processes data is present

tests.description: the seeded favicon is used when present
tests.key: LoadUrl › uses the provided favicon

tests.description: clearing the query closes the overlay
tests.key: Search › hides the overlay when the query is cleared

tests.description: re-indexes when the labels storage key changes, surfacing the new label
tests.key: Search › re-indexes when labels change

tests.description: the resting search box renders its input
tests.key: Search › renders the search input

tests.description: a query with no matches still opens the overlay in its No Results state
tests.key: Search › shows No Results for an unmatched query

tests.description: a label-matching query surfaces the label under Groups
tests.key: Search › shows a matching label in the results

tests.description: a url-matching query surfaces the labeled url under Grouped URLs
tests.key: Search › shows a matching labeled url in the results

tests.description: Enter on the selected label activates it: selects the label and routes Home
tests.key: SearchResults › activates a label on Enter

tests.description: the Archived URLs affordance is always present, even with results
tests.key: SearchResults › always renders the Archived URLs section

tests.description: ArrowDown moves the selection from the first item to the next
tests.key: SearchResults › moves the selection down on ArrowDown

tests.description: the edit pencil routes to the URL detail page for that url
tests.key: SearchResults › navigates to the URL page from the edit affordance

tests.description: with no labels and no urls the overlay shows a No Results state
tests.key: SearchResults › renders No Results when there are no matches

tests.description: a match landing in notes renders the highlighted snippet
tests.key: SearchResults › renders a highlighted notes snippet when the match is in notes

tests.description: label hits render under the Groups section
tests.key: SearchResults › renders label hits under Groups

tests.description: url hits render under the Grouped URLs section with their title
tests.key: SearchResults › renders url hits under Grouped URLs

tests.description: Browser-pinned tabs are filtered out of the Active Tabs list.
tests.key: Tabs › excludes browser-pinned tabs from the active list

tests.description: Labels split tabs into grouped headings plus an Ungrouped remainder.
tests.key: Tabs › groups labeled tabs under headings and leaves the rest ungrouped

tests.description: Each seeded active tab renders as a row showing its stored title.
tests.key: Tabs › renders a row for each seeded active tab

tests.description: With no active tabs, the Active Tabs section shows its empty-state explainer copy.
tests.key: Tabs › shows the empty-state explainer when there are no active tabs

tests.description: The History section is collapsed by default and expands when its title is clicked.
tests.key: Tabs › toggles the History section open when its title is clicked

tests.description: With no stored title, the row displays the URL derived from urlKey.
tests.key: Url › derives the display url from urlKey when there is no title

tests.description: With zero process samples, the load indicator is hidden entirely.
tests.key: Url › hides the load indicator when there are no process samples

tests.description: Without a urlKey, Url renders an empty node (no .Url row).
tests.key: Url › renders nothing meaningful without a urlKey

tests.description: Renders the stored title and a favicon image for a seeded url.
tests.key: Url › renders the title and favicon for a seeded url

tests.description: Hovering the row reveals the action row (edit and other actions).
tests.key: Url › reveals the action row on hover

tests.description: Process stats above the excessive thresholds render the excessive load indicator.
tests.key: Url › shows the excessive load indicator for heavy process stats

tests.description: Light process stats render the low load indicator.
tests.key: Url › shows the low load indicator for light process stats

tests.description: Medium-range process stats render the medium load indicator.
tests.key: Url › shows the medium load indicator for moderate process stats

tests.description: saving with the notes field left empty omits the notes key entirely
tests.key: UrlDetails › omits notes when the field is left empty

tests.description: a Groups chip renders for every label that contains this urlKey, and only those
tests.key: UrlDetails › renders a chip per label that contains the url

tests.description: an unlabeled url renders no Groups chips
tests.key: UrlDetails › renders no chips when the url belongs to no label

tests.description: the seeded title/url/favicon/notes hydrate the form fields
tests.key: UrlDetails › renders the stored url fields into the form

tests.description: editing notes and saving persists the four form fields and navigates home
tests.key: UrlDetails › saves the edited url and returns home

tests.description: typing into the field forwards each keystroke to onChange
tests.key: UrlField › calls onChange as the user types

tests.description: renders the label text and the value inside a text input by default
tests.key: UrlField › renders a labeled text input carrying the value

tests.description: multiline renders a textarea instead of an input, with the name-derived class
tests.key: UrlField › renders a textarea when multiline is set

tests.description: clicking the chip calls onRemove so the page can remove the url from the group
tests.key: UrlLabel › calls onRemove when clicked

tests.description: the close icon is rendered alongside the title
tests.key: UrlLabel › renders a remove icon

tests.description: renders the group title in a button whose value carries the title
tests.key: UrlLabel › renders the label title

tests.description: moving a url out of a group whose member is not an open tab needs no ungroup
tests.key: applyDrag › does not report an ungroup when the moved url has no active tab

tests.description: a url dragged from the Tabs sidebar (a non-label source) is inserted into the destination label at the drop index, with nothing to ungroup
tests.key: applyDrag › inserts a url dropped from the sidebar into the destination label

tests.description: moving a url between two groups removes it from the source group's urlKeys, inserts it into the destination at the drop index, and reports the active tab id that must be ungrouped from its Chrome tab group
tests.key: applyDrag › moves a url between groups and reports the tab to ungroup

tests.description: a drop with no valid destination is a no-op (returns null)
tests.key: applyDrag › returns null when there is no destination

tests.description: reordering a group rewrites every label's position to its new index
tests.key: applyDrag › rewrites positions when a group is reordered

tests.description: every labeled urlKey maps back to its label title

tests.description: one document per label, carrying the prefixed id, title, and color
tests.key: buildSearchDocuments › builds one label document per label

tests.key: buildSearchDocuments › returns empty outputs for no labels

tests.description: a label with no urlKeys is tolerated and contributes no map entries
tests.key: buildSearchDocuments › tolerates a label with missing urlKeys

tests.description: each labeled url becomes a document tagged with its label title
tests.key: buildUrlDocuments › builds one document per labeled url

tests.description: an empty labelMap yields no documents
tests.key: buildUrlDocuments › returns no documents for an empty labelMap

tests.description: a url with no backing record is skipped rather than throwing

tests.description: with no seeded processes the listener stays a no-op (table renders empty)

tests.description: action stubs are callable and no-op (resolve callbacks / empty queries)
tests.key: chromeShim › exposes callable no-op action stubs

tests.description: get accepts the string, array, and null (all) key forms
tests.key: chromeShim › get supports string, array, and all forms

tests.description: dynamic per-URL keys (not in KNOWN_KEYS) hydrate too — without this, every tab would render blank because the url-_url_ objects would never load
tests.key: chromeShim › hydrates dynamic url-_url_ keys that are not in KNOWN_KEYS

tests.description: seeded localStorage hydrates the in-memory store on install
tests.key: chromeShim › hydrates the store from seeded localStorage

tests.description: a malformed seed value is ignored rather than crashing the boot
tests.key: chromeShim › ignores malformed seed values

tests.description: install is inert when a real extension chrome.storage.local already exists
tests.key: chromeShim › is inert when a real chrome.storage.local is present

tests.description: remove clears the key from both the store and localStorage and fires onChanged
tests.key: chromeShim › remove clears the key and fires onChanged with the old value

tests.description: removeListener detaches a previously-added listener

tests.description: set merges into the store, mirrors to localStorage, and fires onChanged
tests.key: chromeShim › set mirrors to localStorage and fires onChanged with areaName local

tests.description: the known-keys list is the shared TabCommand storage contract
tests.key: chromeShim › shares the known TabCommand storage keys

tests.description: cpu present without memory fields reads memory as zero

tests.description: a full record maps cpu straight through and sums the two memory fields

tests.key: deriveGaugeTotals › returns zeros for an empty record

tests.description: only one memory field present still produces a numeric sum
tests.key: deriveGaugeTotals › sums when only one memory field is present

tests.description: a null/undefined record is tolerated and reads as zero
tests.key: deriveGaugeTotals › tolerates a missing record
deriveUrlLabels › returns a single title when only one label matches
deriveUrlLabels › returns an empty array for empty or undefined labels
deriveUrlLabels › returns an empty array when no label matches

tests.description: memory at base + half-max leaves half empty

tests.description: a full gauge (value at base + max) leaves 0% empty
tests.key: gaugeFillPercent › returns 0 when value reaches base + max

tests.key: gaugeFillPercent › returns 100 when value equals base

tests.description: a half-loaded gauge leaves half the segments empty
tests.key: gaugeFillPercent › returns 50 at the half-way point

tests.description: values above the ceiling go negative (un-clamped, mirrors the reference)
tests.key: gaugeFillPercent › returns a negative percent when value exceeds base + max

tests.description: the base floor is subtracted before computing the fraction
tests.key: gaugeFillPercent › subtracts the base before computing the fraction
